records.ety: [F. injustice, L. injustitia. See In- not, and Justice, and cf. Unjust.]

defs: Lack of justice and equity; violation of the rights of another or others; iniquity; wrong; unfairness; imposition.
quotes: If this people [the Athenians] resembled Nero in their extravagance, much more did they resemble and even exceed him in cruelty and injustice.

defs: An unjust act or deed; a sin; a crime; a wrong.
quotes: Cunning men can be guilty of a thousand injustices without being discovered, or at least without being punished.
